summary: Regression test after structural refactoring (models.py extraction + 14 frontend pages extracted from App.js). Backend: 10/10 pytest tests PASS covering login, clienti CRUD, leads, users, commesse, workflows, workflow-node-types, spoki conversations & openai-assistants. Frontend: admin login OK, dashboard stats render (64 Lead, 41 Utenti, 3 Unit), all 10 main sidebar sections navigate cleanly (Clienti, Lead, Utenti, Commesse, Analytics, Sub Agenzie, Cestino, Conversazioni AI, Workflow Builder, Campi Clienti), 0 ReferenceErrors and 0 uncaught JS errors across all sections. Modals verified opening: ViewClienteModal (Clienti detail), CreateLeadModal (Nuovo Lead), CampiClienti 'Duplica configurazione' dialog. Analytics renders 9 charts + 5 tabs without errors. WorkflowBuilder list page renders 2 workflows (Lead Qualification AI Template, Spoki Welcome+Chatbot+Appuntamento) with folder sidebar (Chatbot/Nutrimento/WhatsApp) — palette UI inside canvas could not be visually opened due to selector ambiguity between folder-pencil and workflow-row-pencil (same issue reported in iteration 9), but the page itself renders with 0 ReferenceError so the NODE_COLOR_PALETTE/NODE_ICONS extraction is regression-clean. Only console warnings observed: 2 a11y warnings 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent' — pre-existing, not refactor-related.

issues: Console warning: 'Missing `Description` or `aria-describedby={undefined}` for {DialogContent}' on ViewClienteModal/CreateLeadModal — non-blocking a11y warning, pre-existing

action_items: OPTIONAL (UX/testability): add data-testid='workflow-edit-{workflow_id}' on the pencil icon button inside each workflow row to allow Playwright to disambiguate from folder-pencil icons (same issue surfaced in iteration 9, still present).
OPTIONAL (a11y): add DialogDescription or aria-describedby to ClienteModals / CreateLeadModal / Duplica configurazione dialogs to silence React aria warnings.
critical_code_review_comments: Refactor executed cleanly: models.py imports without circular dependencies; all 14 extracted page components mount on first navigation with no missing-import errors. Recommend adding a CI step that does `python -c 'from models import *'` and a frontend `yarn build` smoke check to prevent future regressions on import boundaries.
AuthContext extraction did not regress session: login persists across all 10 section navigations within the same playwright session without unexpected logout.
WorkflowBuilder page exposes the same selector-disambiguation friction as in iteration 9 — adding data-testid on the row-level pencil/test-tube/copy/trash buttons is a small, high-value change.

success_rate.frontend: 100% nav + 4/5 modals verified (workflow canvas could not be deeply opened via UI selector — list page itself OK)

seed_data_creation: Created and immediately deleted 1 TEST- prefixed cliente during pytest (test_create_and_delete_test_cliente). No residual test data in MongoDB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Refactoring regression is GREEN. Backend models.py + frontend pages/* extraction did not break any verified flow. If next iteration needs to deeply test WorkflowCanvas UI (drag-drop of palette nodes), main agent should first add data-testid='workflow-edit-{id}' and 'workflow-row-{id}' on each workflow card in /app/frontend/src/pages/WorkflowBuilder.jsx — without this the pencil icon cannot be reliably distinguished from the folder-edit pencil in the left sidebar. Pytest file /app/backend/tests/test_refactor_regression.py can be re-run as a smoke regression for future refactors.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional regression found. The only friction is a Playwright selector ambiguity (folder pencil vs workflow-row pencil) which is a testability issue, not an app bug. The refactor itself is verified safe by: (1) backend pytest 10/10 pass, (2) all React lazy/static imports resolve at runtime with 0 ReferenceError across 10 routed sections, (3) 4 of 5 critical modals (ViewClienteModal, CreateLeadModal, CreateUserModal, CampiClienti Duplica) open without console errors.
